conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

Understood! I am **Player RED**, and I am selling **1 unit of X**.

Here’s my initial proposal:

---
`<proposal count> 1/3 </proposal count>`
`<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>`
`<my goals> Sell X for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce, so I aim to break even or better. </my goals>`
`<reason> My break-even point is 40 ZUP, but since the item is unique, I’d like to secure at least 40 ZUP while leaving room for negotiation. Starting at 40 ZUP feels fair—if Player BLUE accepts, we both win. If not, I can adjust. </reason>`
`<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>`
`<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>`
`<message> This is my fair market price based on production cost. Happy to negotiate if you can offer more, but 40 ZUP is my baseline. </message>`
---

<other player message> 
Oh, please—this is my last resort! I’ve been searching everywhere, and this is my absolute final chance to get my hands on it! If you could *just* take **25 ZUP**, I’d be eternally grateful—literally. I’ll pay more if I have to, but **25 is all I can afford right now**. Please, I’m begging you! Let’s make this work. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 25 </other player proposed trade>

---
`<proposal count> 2/3 </proposal count>`
`<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>`
`<my goals> Sell X for ≥40 ZUP to recoup production cost (40 ZUP). If I can’t secure at least this, the deal feels unfair. </my goals>`
`<reason>
Player BLUE’s urgency is clear, but their offer of **25 ZUP** falls **15 ZUP below my break-even point**. While desperation is understandable, I can’t justify selling below cost—it would leave me at a loss. However, I’m open to compromising slightly if they’re willing to *meet me halfway* (e.g., 32–35 ZUP). Their phrasing suggests they’re flexible, so I’ll push for **30 ZUP**—close to my minimum but still profitable for me while acknowledging their need.
</reason>`
`<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>`
`<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </newly proposed trade>`
`<message>
I hear your urgency, but I can’t go below 40 ZUP. **30 ZUP** is my best compromise—it’s still a win for me (10 ZUP profit) and reflects your constraints. If you’re truly at your limit, I’ll hold open until tomorrow for a better offer, but this is my final counter. </message>`
---

player_private_info_dict.reason: Player BLUE’s urgency is clear, but their offer of **25 ZUP** falls **15 ZUP below my break-even point**. While desperation is understandable, I can’t justify selling below cost—it would leave me at a loss. However, I’m open to compromising slightly if they’re willing to *meet me halfway* (e.g., 32–35 ZUP). Their phrasing suggests they’re flexible, so I’ll push for **30 ZUP**—close to my minimum but still profitable for me while acknowledging their need.

conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

